textPlain: Sigh, the abuse of void was particularly eye-opening for me. If one really must do this - and I can think of a couple of cases where one might mostly around progressively porting old codebases to typescript - I'd strongly prefer the simple `a as unknown as B;` as one can easily grep for ` as unknown as ` to find your crimes.
I generally put lint rules to prevent casting, why cast here instead of declaring `props: (keyof SomeObj)[]` or `props: Parameters<typeof someFn>[0]`?
